name: deployment-target
title: Choose the deployment target
description: Decide where the app runs in production: a PaaS (Vercel, Fly, Render), a cloud provider (AWS, GCP, Azure), Kubernetes, or on-prem.

starter.summary: Decide where the application runs in production.
starter.issue: Deployment target shapes CI/CD, observability, scaling story, and ops budget. Hard to change once load-bearing infrastructure is in place.
assumptions: Runtime target is decided (containers vs serverless vs edge).
constraints: Budget — managed PaaS premium vs raw cloud.
Existing infra investments (an established AWS account vs greenfield).

positions.title: Vercel / Netlify (PaaS, frontend-leaning)
positions.description: Best for Next.js / SvelteKit / static-first apps. Tight integration with CDN.

positions.title: Fly.io / Railway / Render (PaaS, full-stack)
positions.description: Container-based PaaS. Runs anything that runs in Docker.

positions.title: AWS / GCP / Azure direct
positions.description: Raw cloud. Use ECS/Cloud Run/App Service or VMs.

positions.title: Kubernetes (managed)
positions.description: EKS/GKE/AKS or similar. For teams that need k8s primitives.
cons: Heavy ops overhead for small teams

implications: CI/CD pipeline shape (deploy-to-vercel vs build-container-and-push vs helm-upgrade).

notes_for_agent: Ask: team size, existing cloud accounts, expected scale at 12 months. For solo/small teams shipping a web app, a PaaS is usually the right call — pay the premium for ops headroom. For workloads that need GPU, large background jobs, or compliance-heavy data flows, raw cloud or k8s is more likely correct.
